hooks.command: python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); cmd=d.get('tool_input',{}).get('command',''); r={}; ('git commit' in cmd or 'git push' in cmd) and r.update({'hookSpecificOutput':{'hookEventName':'PreToolUse','additionalContext':'PRE-COMMIT CHECK (from pre-commit-checks skill): 1) Verify go build passes 2) Verify go test passes (0 FAIL) 3) New technique packages have doc.go with MITRE ID, detection level, how-it-works, example 4) New packages added to README table + docs/ page + docs/mitre.md 5) No credentials (gho_*, ghp_*, sk-*, AKIA*, private keys) in staged files — run: git diff --cached | grep -iE gho_|ghp_|sk-|AKIA|BEGIN.PRIVATE 6) ignore/ folder NOT staged — run: git diff --cached --name-only | grep ^ignore/ 7) No naming violations (stuttering, Get-prefix) 8) README doc links all valid. BLOCK commit if any CRITICAL check fails.'}}); print(json.dumps(r))"

python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); cmd=d.get('tool_input',{}).get('command',''); r={}; 'git push' in cmd and r.update({'hookSpecificOutput':{'hookEventName':'PostToolUse','additionalContext':'POST-PUSH: Verify GitHub repo description and topics are current. If this is a release, consider tagging with SEMVER (git tag -a vX.Y.Z).'}}); print(json.dumps(r))"

python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); fp=d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''); r={}; fp.endswith('.go') and r.update({'hookSpecificOutput':{'hookEventName':'PostToolUse','additionalContext':'POST-WRITE GO CHECK (mandatory): You just modified a .go file. Run these checks NOW before continuing: 1) NAMING: camelCase/PascalCase, ID not Id, no stuttering (pkg.NewPkg->pkg.New AND pkg.Pkg->pkg.Info), no Get prefix, main type must not repeat package name 2) OS API: verify return semantics (NTSTATUS!=0=err vs BOOL==0=err vs HANDLE==0=err), param count matches MSDN, handles closed with defer, String() on Windows enums returns MSDN names 3) DESIGN PATTERNS: scan for signals — big switch->Strategy, duplication->Decorator/Template, 5+ field struct->Builder, state-dependent behavior->State, monitoring->Observer+Event channel 4) TESTS: LIST every new/modified exported symbol by name, then NAME the test for each — if you cannot name the test, write it NOW 5) PARAMETRIZATION: no new hardcoded timeouts/paths/iterations that users should control 6) PACKAGE DESIGN: no chan any (use typed Event), OS-native IDs over custom hashes, private state, watchers detect add+remove 7) CALLER SYSCALL: any NtXxx call should accept optional *wsyscall.Caller for EDR bypass — kernel32-only exempt 8) REUSABILITY: any function useful beyond this package MUST be in a shared package (win/ntapi, win/token, inject/, etc.) — not trapped in the consumer. Extract immediately if generic. Fix violations immediately. Silence = compliant.'}}); print(json.dumps(r))"
